title: Radians & arc length
description: Solve problems related to radians and arc length like finding an arc length given the central angle and radius.

assessment_items.question_content:
An arc [[☃ definition 1]] a central angle measuring $\dfrac{7\pi}{4}$radians.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What fraction of the circumference is this arc?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] of the circumference
hints: We're interested in the fraction $\dfrac{\tealE{ \text{arc length}}}{\text{circumference}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\tealE{\text{arc length}}}{\text{circumference}} &= \dfrac{\tealE{\theta \cancel r}}{2\pi \cancel r} \\\\
&= \dfrac{\left(\purpleD{\dfrac{7\pi}{4}}\right)}{2\pi} \\\\
&= \purpleD{\dfrac{7\cancel{\pi}}{4}} \cdot \dfrac{1}{2\cancel{\pi}}\\\\
&= \dfrac{7}{8}
\end{align}$
The arc is $\dfrac{7}{8}$ of the circumference.

options.definition: Reaches from one ray to the other of

options.alt: A circle with a central angle that measures seven pi divided by four radians and the corresponding major arc highlighted. A radius is labeled r.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What fraction of the circumference is this arc?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] of the circumference
hints: We're interested in the fraction $\dfrac{\tealE{ \text{arc length}}}{\text{circumference}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\tealE{\text{arc length}}}{\text{circumference}} &= \dfrac{\tealE{\theta \cancel r}}{2\pi \cancel r} \\\\
&= \dfrac{\left(\purpleD{\dfrac{3\pi}{5}}\right)}{2\pi} \\\\

&= \dfrac{3}{10}
\end{align}$
The arc is $\dfrac{3}{10}$ of the circumference.

assessment_items.question_content:
An arc [[☃ definition 1]] a central angle measuring $\dfrac{\pi}{2}$ radians.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What fraction of the circumference is this arc?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] of the circumference
hints: We're interested in the fraction $\dfrac{\tealE{ \text{arc length}}}{\text{circumference}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\tealE{\text{arc length}}}{\text{circumference}} &= \dfrac{\tealE{\theta \cancel r}}{2\pi \cancel r} \\\\
&= \dfrac{\left(\purpleD{\dfrac{\pi}{2}}\right)}{2\pi} \\\\

&= \dfrac{1}{4}
\end{align}$
The arc is $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of the circumference.

options.definition: Reaches from one ray to the other of

options.alt: A circle with an arc with a central angle of pi over two radians.

answers.message: The ratio of arc length to circumference is $\dfrac{\dfrac{\pi}{2}}{2\pi}$, not $\dfrac{2\pi}{\dfrac{\pi}{2}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
An arc [[☃ definition 1]] a central angle measuring $\dfrac{2\pi}{3}$ radians.



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ input-number 1]] of the circumference
hints: We're interested in the fraction $\dfrac{\tealE{ \text{arc length}}}{\text{circumference}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\tealE{\text{arc length}}}{\text{circumference}} &= \dfrac{\tealE{\theta \cancel r}}{2\pi \cancel r} \\\\

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the measure (in radians) of central angle $\theta$ in the circle below?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ input-number 1]] radians
hints:
A central angle $(\theta)$ measured in radians tells us how many times the radius fits into the arc.

$\theta = \dfrac{\text{arc length}}{\text{radius}}$
$\dfrac{6\,\text{cm}}{4\,\text{cm}} = 1.5$ radians

options.alt: A circle with a radius of four centimeters. There are two radii that highlight a sector. The measure of the arc is six centimeters. The central angle is labeled theta.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the length of arc $S$ shown below?**


[[☃ image 1]]

*The angle in the figure is a central angle in radians.*

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
A central angle $(\theta)$ measured in radians tells us how many times the radius fits into the arc.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\text{arc length}}{\text{radius}} &=\theta\\\\
\text{arc length} &= \theta \cdot \text{radius}\\\\
S&=2 \cdot 7\text{ cm}\\\\
S& = 14\text{ cm}\end{align}$
The length of the arc is $14 \text{ cm}$.

options.alt: A circle with a radius of seven centimeters. There are two radii that highlight a sector. The measure of the arc is S centimeters. The central angle measuring two radians.

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ image 1]]

*The angle in the figure is a central angle in radians.*

hints:
A central angle $(\theta)$ measured in radians tells us how many times the radius fits into the arc.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\text{arc length}}{\text{radius}} &=\theta\\\\
\text{arc length} &= \theta \cdot \text{radius}\\\\
S&=\dfrac{3\pi}{5} \cdot 6\text{ cm}\\\\

The length of the arc is $\dfrac{18\pi}{5} \text{ cm}$.

$S = $ [[☃ expression 1]]

options.alt: A circle with a radius of r centimeters. There are two radii that highlight a sector. The measure of the arc is S centimeters. The central angle measuring five point six radians.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write a formula for $r$ in terms of  $\theta$ for the following figure.**



[[☃ image 1]]

 
*The angle in the figure is a central angle in radians.*

$r = $ [[☃ expression 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
A central angle $(\theta)$ measured in radians tells us how many times the radius fits into the arc.

$\begin{align}
\theta &= \dfrac{\text{arc length}}{\text{radius}}\\\\
\theta &=\dfrac{4\,\text{cm}}{r}&\text{Substitute}.\\\\
\theta\cdot r &=4\,\text{cm}&\text{Multiply by }r.\\\\
r &= \dfrac{4}{\theta}\,\text{cm} & \text{Divide by }\theta.
\end{align}$

The formula is:

$r = \dfrac{4}{\theta}$

options.alt: A circle with a radius of r centimeters. There are two radii that highlight a sector. The measure of the arc is four centimeters. The central angle measuring theta radians.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write a formula for $r$ in terms of  $\theta$ for the following figure.**



[[☃ image 1]]

*The angle in the figure is a central angle in radians.*

$r = $ [[☃ expression 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
A central angle $(\theta)$ measured in radians tells us how many times the radius fits into the arc.

$\begin{align}
\theta &= \dfrac{\text{arc length}}{\text{radius}}\\\\
\theta &=\dfrac{7\pi\,\text{cm}}{r}&\text{Substitute}.\\\\
\theta\cdot r &=7\pi\,\text{cm}&\text{Multiply by }r.\\\\
r &= \dfrac{7\pi}{\theta}\,\text{cm} & \text{Divide by }\theta.
\end{align}$

The formula is:

$r = \dfrac{7\pi}{\theta}$

options.alt: A circle with a radius of r centimeters. There are two radii that highlight a sector. The measure of the arc is seven pi centimeters. The central angle measuring theta radians.
